step_by_step_reasoning: Data from various customer sources (EFS, DynamoDB, Redshift, S3) is aggregated and transformed using AWS Glue. The transformed data is written to a central S3 bucket. This write triggers a Lambda function (Cluster stage), which invokes SageMaker to cluster customer groups. Next, a secondary prediction run (Predict stage) is triggered on SageMaker. SageMaker outputs the 'next best offer' recommendations back into S3. The prediction outputs are finally consumed via APIs for real-time applications or visualized through BI dashboards.

graph.name: TASOLS Event-Driven Serverless Machine Learning Pipeline
graph.notes: A serverless, event-driven machine learning architecture that chains SageMaker models (k-means clustering and random forest prediction) to deliver real-time recommendations and BI dashboards.

nodes.name: S3 Input
nodes.notes: Landing zone for batch CSV and raw data inputs.

nodes.name: Glue
nodes.notes: Performs ETL on incoming source data.

nodes.name: S3 Central
nodes.notes: Stores ready data and triggers the ML pipeline on write events.

nodes.name: SageMaker
nodes.notes: Runs k-means clustering and random forest prediction models.

nodes.name: API Client
nodes.notes: Real-time API endpoints for operational application integration.

nodes.name: BI Dashboard
nodes.notes: Consumes downstream prediction results for analytics.

edges.notes: Loads clean data into central S3.
S3 write event triggers orchestrating Lambda.
Lambda invokes SageMaker training and prediction runs.
Saves generated ML predictions back to S3.
Exposes predicted offers via low-latency API.
Supplies prediction metrics to analytical dashboards.
